source_name: CDC ACIP 2018 pertussis recommendations; FDA INFANRIX; Merck VAXELIS PI
notes: ACIP states DTaP replaced whole-cell doses in 1997; current infant and childhood U.S. products in routine use include PRN-positive DTaP and hexavalent formulations. The selected-country review report extends this sustained acellular PRN-positive background through 2025.

source_name: CDC ACIP 2018 pertussis recommendations
notes: Whole-cell pertussis vaccine was used in the United States before the 1997 DTaP switch; the historical baseline is therefore truncated at 1996 rather than being extended into the mixed DTaP transition.

source_name: CDC 1997 ACIP DTaP recommendations; CDC ACIP 2018 pertussis recommendations
notes: CDC stated in 1997 that DTaP was preferred for all pertussis-containing doses while whole-cell products remained acceptable alternatives during the transition; by 2000 whole-cell vaccines were no longer available in the United States, so 1997-1999 is curated as a mixed transition interval.

source_name: UKHSA hexavalent programme guidance; GOV.UK pertussis vaccine use; EMA Infanrix Hexa; Merck VAXELIS PI
notes: UK infant programme uses Infanrix hexa or Vaxelis and maternal or booster products are acellular pertussis vaccines with PRN-positive formulations

source_name: UKHSA vaccine timeline; pertussis immunisation history review
notes: The UK historical timeline places the primary-schedule acellular switch in 2004. This pre-2004 baseline is truncated to the earliest interpretable historical UK isolates in the current genome panel so the selected-country screen can evaluate a whole-cell epoch against the later acellular programme.

source_name: Health New Zealand immunisation history
notes: Health New Zealand immunisation history identifies August 2000 as the national switch from DTwP to DTaP, so the preceding routine childhood programme is curated as whole-cell and PRN-unexposed for selected-country epoch screening.

source_name: Health New Zealand immunisation history; Medsafe INFANRIX HEXA datasheet; Health NZ school booster standards
notes: Acellular pertussis replaced whole-cell vaccine in August 2000; infant and booster products used in the national schedule are PRN-positive. The selected-country review report extends this row through 2025.

source_name: French Bordetella evolution study
notes: Whole-cell pertussis vaccine was used in France for primovaccination and the first booster until the acellular programme began in 1998; the pre-acellular interval is retained as a whole-cell baseline for selected-country screening.

source_name: French Bordetella evolution study; French 2024 vaccination calendar; EMA Infanrix Hexa; EMA Hexyon; Merck VAXELIS PI
notes: Acellular pertussis was introduced in 1998 as an adolescent booster and then generalized to the whole population; French acellular products include both PT/FHA-only and PT/FHA/PRN formulations, so the programme is curated as mixed-brand heterogeneous rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

source_name: Czech 2018 hexavaccine recommendation; EMA Infanrix hexa
notes: Czech expert guidance states that from 1 January 2018 only Hexacima would be routinely available, while children already started on Infanrix hexa in 2017 should complete the same schedule with Infanrix hexa when possible; the pre-shift 2007-2017 routine hexavalent period is therefore curated as PRN-positive Infanrix-hexa-type exposure

source_name: Czech 2018 hexavaccine recommendation; EMA Hexacima
notes: Czech expert guidance for the 2018-2021 procurement period states that only Hexacima would be available for routine hexavaccine use; EMA product information lists pertussis toxoid and filamentous haemagglutinin without PRN, so this interval is curated as routine aP and PRN-negative

source_name: Czech 2022 reimbursed immunisation calendar; EMA Infanrix hexa; EMA Hexacima
notes: The current Czech reimbursed childhood calendar lists both Infanrix Hexa and Hexacima for the routine hexavalent series; Infanrix hexa is PRN-positive whereas Hexacima lacks PRN, so the current programme is curated as mixed

source_name: RIVM programme-change history; RIVM vaccine information leaflets; RIVM what is in a vaccine page; EMA Vaxelis
notes: RIVM states that whole-cell pertussis vaccine was replaced by acellular pertussis vaccine for children born from 1 January 2005 onward. Current Dutch programme materials list PRN-positive pertussis-containing products such as Vaxelis, so the 2005-2024 routine programme is curated as PRN-positive.

source_name: Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; CDC review of pertactin-deficient expansion
notes: Japan-specific literature reports that five long-used DTaP brands had heterogeneous antigen compositions and only three contained PRN before the 2012 DTaP-IPV change, so the historical pre-2012 acellular period is curated as mixed rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

source_name: National Institute of Public Health Japan report on pertussis-containing vaccine antigen composition; Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; CDC review of pertactin-deficient expansion; PMDA TRIBIK product information; PMDA GOBIK product information
notes: The post-2012 Japanese programme is curated as routine aP without PRN because the published Japan-specific literature ties the 2012 DTaP-IPV change to vaccines lacking PRN, and the current NIPH plus PMDA sources support TRIBIK and GOBIK as the relevant PRN-negative pediatric products.

source_name: Finnish epidemiology study documenting the 2005 replacement of whole-cell vaccine by acellular vaccine
notes: Finnish programme history is curated as whole-cell through 2004 because the country-specific epidemiology literature identifies 2005 as the replacement year for routine whole-cell pertussis vaccination.

source_name: Finnish epidemiology study documenting the 2005 replacement of whole-cell vaccine by acellular vaccine; Finnish antigen-expression study describing PT/FHA-only followed by PT/FHA/PRN acellular vaccine use
notes: Finland replaced whole-cell vaccine in 2005 and the Finnish antigen-expression review describes the first acellular interval as PT and FHA only before PRN was added back in 2009, so 2005-2008 is curated as routine aP and PRN-negative.

source_name: Finnish antigen-expression study describing PT/FHA-only followed by PT/FHA/PRN acellular vaccine use
notes: The Finnish antigen-expression review identifies a 2009 transition to PT, FHA, and PRN acellular vaccine use, so the later routine programme is curated as PRN-positive.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study identifies a WCV period before ACV boosters and later primary-series replacement, so the pre-1998 routine schedule is curated as whole-cell.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study defines 1998-2005 as the transition period to ACV, with ACV boosters introduced in 1998 while WCV remained in the primary series until exclusive ACV use began in 2006.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study identifies 2006-2018 as the period when ACV had completely replaced WCV, so 2006-2012 is curated as routine aP and PRN-positive in aggregate before partial regional PRN-free heterogeneity emerged.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study reports approval in 2013 of a vaccine without PRN for primary vaccination in 5 of 19 regions, so the later national programme is curated as heterogeneous and mixed rather than cleanly PRN-free.

source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
notes: The Australian handbook documents exclusive whole-cell use before the 1997 booster-only acellular transition, so the routine pre-1997 programme is curated as whole-cell.

source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
notes: The Australian handbook documents booster-dose aP use from 1997 before all-dose aP use from 1999, so 1997-1998 is curated as a mixed transition interval.

source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
notes: The Australian handbook states that routine acellular vaccines used in the national programme contain three or more purified pertussis components including PRN, so the post-1999 routine programme is curated as PRN-positive.

source_name: India Immunization Handbook for Medical Officers 2017; CDSCO DTwP Biological E SmPC; CDSCO Liquid Pentavalent (Bio E) SmPC
notes: The UIP handbook schedules pentavalent primary doses and DPT boosters in the public programme, and CDSCO SmPCs identify both DTwP and liquid pentavalent DTwP-rHepB-Hib products as whole-cell pertussis vaccines; the routine public childhood series is therefore curated as whole-cell and PRN-unexposed

source_name: China CDC pertussis vaccine immunoprophylaxis page
notes: China CDC states that pertussis-containing whole-cell vaccine use began in the 1960s and that wP entered the planned immunization programme in 1978. This row uses 1978 as the national routine-programme anchor for the whole-cell era before DTaP was marketed.

source_name: China CDC pertussis vaccine immunoprophylaxis page; Emerging Infectious Diseases review of China vaccine transition
notes: China CDC states that DTaP was marketed in 2005, incorporated into the National Immunization Program in 2007, and fully replaced DTwP in 2012. The 2005-2011 interval is therefore curated as a mixed transition period with concurrent whole-cell and acellular exposure.

source_name: China CDC pertussis vaccine immunoprophylaxis page; Shenzhen Kangtai DTaP-Hib-IPV product page; recent China vaccine genomics and review papers
notes: China CDC states that DTaP had completely replaced DTwP by 2012. Current Chinese acellular products and recent reviews indicate heterogeneous antigen composition, including PRN-positive component or co-purified vaccines and PRN-negative two-component vaccines, so post-2012 country-level PRN exposure is curated as mixed rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

source_name: South Africa EPI vaccinator manual; WHO and Sanofi Hexaxim product information
notes: South African infant programme switched to Pentaxim and Hexaxim-type acellular products with PT and FHA but no PRN; this curation prioritizes the routine childhood formulation present in the ecological panel year

source_name: Brazil Ministry of Health dTpa page; 2024 national schedule instruction; Ministry pages for maternal dTpa use
notes: Brazil retains whole-cell DTP and DTP-Hib-HepB primary and childhood booster products in the public schedule while also using adult acellular dTpa in pregnancy; Ministry of Health materials state that gestational dTpa has been offered since 2014, so country-level PRN exposure is coded mixed from 2014 onward.

source_name: PAHO and MSPAS Guatemala vaccination dashboard launch; WHO-prequalified DTwP-HepB-Hib package insert; PAHO notice on Guatemala hexavalent introduction
notes: PAHO and MSPAS identify Pentavalente and DPT as routine childhood pertussis-containing vaccines in Guatemala, and the November 2025 PAHO and MSPAS launch of hexavalent indicates that this switch occurred after the analytic GTM row in 2014. The WHO-prequalified DTwP-HepB-Hib package insert specifies whole-cell pertussis vaccine in the pentavalent class, so the pre-hexavalent public schedule is curated as whole-cell and PRN-unexposed with high-confidence class-level support
